name: qandle

metadata.name: qandle
metadata.summary: QANDLE is a fast and simple quantum state-vector simulator for hybrid machine learning using the PyTorch library.

documentation:
# QANDLE
**QANDLE** is a fast and simple quantum state-vector simulator for hybrid machine learning using the PyTorch library.
Documentation and examples can be found in the [QANDLE documentation](https://gstenzel.github.io/qandle/), the code resides on [GitHub](https://github.com/gstenzel/qandle).
The paper can be found on [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.09213).

## Installation
```bash
pip install qandle
```

## Usage
```python
import torch
import qandle

# Create a quantum circuit
circuit = qandle.Circuit(
    layers=[
        # embedding layer
        qandle.AngleEmbedding(num_qubits=2),
        # trainable layer, with random initialization
        qandle.RX(qubit=1),
        # trainable layer, with fixed initialization
        qandle.RY(qubit=0, theta=torch.tensor(0.2)),
        # data reuploading
        qandle.RX(qubit=0, name="data_reuploading"),
        # disable quantum weight remapping
        qandle.RY(qubit=1, remapping=None),
        qandle.CNOT(control=0, target=1),
        qandle.MeasureProbability(),
    ]
)


input_state = torch.rand(circuit.num_qubits, dtype=torch.float) # random input
data_reuploading = torch.rand(1, dtype=torch.float) # random data reuploading input

# Run the circuit
circuit(input_state, data_reuploading=data_reuploading)
```	

## License
This project is licensed under the MIT License - see the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.

## Citation
If you use QANDLE in your research, please cite the following paper:
```bbl
@misc{qandle2024,
      title={Qandle: Accelerating State Vector Simulation Using Gate-Matrix Caching and Circuit Splitting}, 
      author={Gerhard Stenzel and Sebastian Zielinski and Michael Kölle and Philipp Altmann and Jonas Nüßlein and Thomas Gabor},
      year={2024},
      eprint={2404.09213},
      archivePrefix={arXiv},
      primaryClass={quant-ph}
}
```

classes.name: qandle.errors.UnbuiltGateError
documentation: Raised when a gate is used in a circuit before it is built. Either build the gate manually using {code}`gate.build()` or use the gate in a circuit, which will build it automatically.

functions.name: qandle.test.test_ansaetze.test_sel
documentation: Test StronglyEntanglingLayers

functions.name: qandle.utils.reduce_dot
documentation: Compute the dot product of a series of matrices.

functions.name: qandle.utils.get_matrix_transforms
documentation: Get einops layers for transforming between state and matrix representation of a subcircuit.

functions.name: qandle.utils.__analyze_barren_plateu
documentation:
Analyze, whether a circuit has a barren plateau for a given loss function,
by checking the gradient of the loss function for num_points parameters (while keeping the rest fixed).

modules.name: qandle.ansaetze
documentation: ruff: noqa: F403 F401

variables.name: qandle.ansaetze.SU
documentation: alias

classes.name: qandle.ansaetze.ansatz.UnbuiltAnsatz
documentation: An ansatz exposed to the user. Needs to be built before it can be used.

classes.name: qandle.ansaetze.ansatz.BuiltAnsatz
documentation: A built ansatz, ready to be used.

classes.name: qandle.ansaetze.stronglyentangling_budget.StronglyEntanglingLayerBudget
documentation:
A strongly entangling layer, inspired by [this paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/1804.00633).
Consists of a series of single-qubit rotations on each qubit specified in the {code}`qubits` parameter, followed by a series of control gates. Instead of specifying a depth, a {code}`param_budget` is specified, which determines the number of parameters in the circuit. The control gates are inserted every {code}`control_gate_spacing` layers, quickly allowing to change the entanglement structure of the circuit.

methods.name: qandle.qcircuit.Circuit.__matmul__
documentation: Allows the circuit to be multiplied with a state tensor, directly calling forward. Not compatible with named inputs.

methods.name: qandle.qcircuit.Circuit.draw
documentation: Shorthand for drawer.draw(self.circuit)

inner_classes.name: qandle.qcircuit.Circuit.DummyUnbuiltCircuit
documentation: Helper class to decompose a circuit with subcircuits without building it.

classes.name: qandle.qcircuit.UnsplittedCircuit

methods.name: qandle.qcircuit.UnsplittedCircuit.__init__
documentation: Create a new quantum circuit, building all layers.

methods.name: qandle.qcircuit.UnsplittedCircuit.forward
documentation: Run the circuit. If state is None, the initial state is used. Supply specific inputs to specific gates using the kwargs, of the form name:torch.Tensor

methods.name: qandle.qcircuit.UnsplittedCircuit.decompose
documentation: Decompose the circuit into a circuit without any subcircuits, returning a new, flat Circuit.

methods.name: qandle.qcircuit.UnsplittedCircuit.split
documentation: Split the circuit into subcircuits, returning a CuttedCircuit if the circuit was split, or the original circuit if not.

methods.name: qandle.qcircuit.UnsplittedCircuit.to_matrix
documentation: Get the matrix representation of the circuit.
Main class for quantum circuits. This class is a torch.nn.Module, so it can be used like any other PyTorch module.

classes.name: qandle.qcircuit.SplittedCircuit
documentation: Circuit with subcircuits, created by splitting a Circuit.

classes.name: qandle.qcircuit.Subcircuit

methods.name: qandle.qcircuit.Subcircuit.__init__
documentation:
num_qubits_parent: number of qubits in the parent circuit
mapping: mapping of qubits from parent circuit to subcircuit
layers: layers of the subcircuit
Submodule for CuttedCircuit, contains a single subcircuits

classes.name: qandle.ansaetze.twolocal.TwoLocal
documentation: The 2-local circuit as described in [the Qiskit docs](https://docs.quantum.ibm.com/api/qiskit/qiskit.circuit.library.TwoLocal). It comprises alternating layers of single qubit rotations and CNOT gates. This implementation uses the {code}`linear` entanglement map.

modules.name: qandle
documentation: ruff: noqa: F403 F401

classes.name: qandle.ansaetze.specialunitary.SpecialUnitary
documentation: The hardware efficient SU(2) 2-local circuit as described in `the Qiskit docs <https://docs.quantum.ibm.com/api/qiskit/qiskit.circuit.library.EfficientSU2>`_.

classes.name: qandle.ansaetze.stronglyentangling.StronglyEntanglingLayer
documentation:
A strongly entangling layer, inspired by [this paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/1804.00633).
Consists of a series of single-qubit rotations on each qubit specified in the {code}`qubits` parameter, followed by a series of CNOT gates. A higher {code}`depth` will increase the expressivity of the circuit at the cost of more parameters and a linear increase in runtime.

functions.name: qandle.qasm.convert_to_qasm
documentation: Convert a circuit to qasm

classes.name: qandle.qasm.QasmRepresentation
documentation: A class representing a single gate in a quantum circuit

variables.name: qandle.measurements.MeasureJointProbability
documentation: alias

methods.name: qandle.measurements.BuiltMeasurement.to_matrix
documentation: No-op for measurements.

classes.name: qandle.measurements.MeasureAllAbsolute
documentation: Measure the absolute probability of the state-vector.

classes.name: qandle.measurements.MeasureState
documentation: Measure the state-vector. As the state-vector is already a known, this measurement is a no-op.

classes.name: qandle.measurements.MeasureProbability
documentation:
Measure the probability of each qubit being in the $\ket{0}$ state.
Use {code}`1 - measure_probability` to get the probability of each qubit being in the $\ket{1}$ state.

functions.name: qandle.splitter.main._assign_to_subcircuits
documentation: From the list of nodes (which have their new group assigned), sort the original layers into subcircuits

functions.name: qandle.splitter.main._normalize_subcircuits
documentation:
subcircuits may have gaps in the qubit indices, remove those e.g.
[CNOT(0,1), CNOT(4,5)] becomes [CNOT(0,1), CNOT(2,3)].
Also returns a mapping from the original index to the normalised index
(so {0:0, 1:1, 4:2, 5:3} in the example above)

class_variables.name: qandle.splitter.main.SubcircuitContainer.layers
documentation: list of gates

class_variables.name: qandle.splitter.main.SubcircuitContainer.mapping
documentation: original index -> normalised index

functions.name: qandle.drawer.draw
documentation: Draw a circuit diagram. This is just a simple text-based drawing, for more advanced drawing, export to OpenQASM and use more advanced tools, like PennyLane's or Qiskit's drawer. For more options, see {class}`qandle.config`.

functions.name: qandle.drawer._format_splitted
documentation:
returns a formatted string of the subcircuits in sub_drawing
if how == "column", the subcircuits are drawn next to each other
if how == "row", the subcircuits are drawn below each other

variables.name: qandle.config.DEFAULT_MAPPING
documentation:
Default weight remapping function for quantum gates. This function limits the weights of the variational quantum circuit's gates.
{code}`qw_map.tanh` is the default value, yielding better results in practice. For higher performance or naive usage, set {code}`DEFAULT_MAPPING = lambda x: x` or {code}`DEFAULT_MAPPING = qw_map.none`. For more information, see the [arxiv paper 1](https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.14807) and [arxiv paper 2](https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.05776).

variables.name: qandle.config.DRAW_SPLITTED_PAD
documentation: Number of spaces between columns in the drawer for splitted circuits.

variables.name: qandle.config.DRAW_DASH
documentation:
Character used for drawing qubits, can be changed to the normal dash '-', depending on font, where {code}`───` often looks better than {code}`---`

```python
>>> qandle.config.DRAW_DASH = "─"
>>> print(circuit.draw())
q0─RZ─RY─RZ─■─────X─RZ─RY─RZ─■───X───
q1─RZ─RY─RZ─X─■───┼─RZ─RY─RZ─┼─■─┼─X─
q2─RZ─RY─RZ───X─■─┼─RZ─RY─RZ─X─┼─■─┼─
q3─RZ─RY─RZ─────X─■─RZ─RY─RZ───X───■─

>>> qandle.config.DRAW_DASH = "-"
>>> print(circuit.draw())
q0-RZ-RY-RZ-■-----X-RZ-RY-RZ-■---X---
q1-RZ-RY-RZ-X-■---┼-RZ-RY-RZ-┼-■-┼-X-
q2-RZ-RY-RZ---X-■-┼-RZ-RY-RZ-X-┼-■-┼-
q3-RZ-RY-RZ-----X-■-RZ-RY-RZ---X---■-
```

variables.name: qandle.config.DRAW_SHOW_VALUES
documentation: Whether to show the values of the gates in the drawer. If not, only the gate positions are shown, reducing clutter.

variables.name: qandle.config.DRAW_SHIFT_LEFT
documentation:
Whether to shift the gates to the left if possible for a more compact drawing.

```python
>>> qandle.config.DRAW_SHIFT_LEFT = False
>>> print(circuit.draw())
q0─RZ─RY─RZ───────────────────■───X─RZ─RY─RZ───────────────────■─X───
q1──────────RZ─RY─RZ──────────X─■─┼──────────RZ─RY─RZ──────────┼─■─X─
q2───────────────────RZ─RY─RZ───X─■───────────────────RZ─RY─RZ─X───■─

>>> qandle.config.DRAW_SHIFT_LEFT = True
>>> print(circuit.draw())
q0─RZ─RY─RZ─■───X─RZ─RY─RZ─■─X───
q1─RZ─RY─RZ─X─■─┼─RZ─RY─RZ─┼─■─X─
q2─RZ─RY─RZ───X─■─RZ─RY─RZ─X───■─
```

variables.name: qandle.config.DRAW_CROSS_BETWEEN_CNOT
documentation:
Whether to draw a cross {code}`┼` on the qubits between the CNOT control and target

```python
>>> qandle.config.DRAW_CROSS_BETWEEN_CNOT = True
>>> print(circuit.draw())
q0─RZ─RY─RZ─■─────X─RZ─RY─RZ─■───X───
q1─RZ─RY─RZ─X─■───┼─RZ─RY─RZ─┼─■─┼─X─
q2─RZ─RY─RZ───X─■─┼─RZ─RY─RZ─X─┼─■─┼─
q3─RZ─RY─RZ─────X─■─RZ─RY─RZ───X───■─

>>> qandle.config.DRAW_CROSS_BETWEEN_CNOT = False
>>> print(circuit.draw())
q0─RZ─RY─RZ─■─────X─RZ─RY─RZ─■───X───
q1─RZ─RY─RZ─X─■─────RZ─RY─RZ───■───X─
q2─RZ─RY─RZ───X─■───RZ─RY─RZ─X───■───
q3─RZ─RY─RZ─────X─■─RZ─RY─RZ───X───■─
```

variables.name: qandle.operators.CustomGate
documentation:
Define a custom single qubit gate. 
This module is always built, and therefore might have a big memory footprint. 

Attributes:
    qubit : int
        The index of the qubit this gate operates on.
    matrix : torch.Tensor
        The matrix representation of the gate. Must be a 2x2 unitary matrix.
    num_qubits : int
        The number of qubits in the circuit.
    self_description : str
        The description of the gate. Default is "U". This name will be used in the string representation of the gate and in the OpenQASM2 representation.

classes.name: qandle.operators.Operator

documentation: Returns a string representation of the operator.

methods.name: qandle.operators.Operator.to_qasm
documentation: Returns the OpenQASM2 representation of the operator.
Everything that can be applied to a state.

documentation: Builds the operator, i.e. converts it to a torch.nn.Module.
Container class for operators that have not been built yet.

documentation: Applies the operator to the state.

methods.name: qandle.operators.BuiltOperator.to_matrix
documentation: Returns the matrix representation of the operator, such that {code}`state @ matrix` is equivalent to {code}`forward(state)`. Might be significantly slower than forward.
Container class for operators that have been built.

documentation: Creates a parametrized operator.
Container class for parametrized operators that have not been built yet.

methods.name: qandle.operators.BuiltParametrizedOperator.matrix_builder
documentation: Returns the matrix builder for the gate.

classes.name: qandle.operators.RX
documentation:
Parametrized RX gate, i.e. a rotation around the x-axis of the Bloch sphere.

This class represents a parametrized RX gate in a quantum circuit.

Used by {class}`qandle.qcircuit.QCircuit` to build the circuit.

Attributes:
: qubit

  : The index of the qubit this gate operates on.

  theta

  : The parameter of the RX gate, by default None. If None, a random parameter $[0, 1]$ is chosen.

  name

  : The name of the operator, by default None. If None, the operator is not named and does not accept named inputs.

  remapping

  : A function that remaps the parameter theta, by default config.DEFAULT_MAPPING. To disable remapping, pass {code}`qw_map.none` or {code}`lambda x: x`.

classes.name: qandle.operators.RY
documentation:
Parametrized RY gate, i.e. a rotation around the y-axis of the Bloch sphere.

This class represents a parametrized RY gate in a quantum circuit.

Used by {class}`qandle.qcircuit.QCircuit` to build the circuit.

Attributes
: qubit

  : The index of the qubit this gate operates on.

  theta

  : The parameter of the RY gate, by default None. If None, a random parameter $[0, 1]$ is chosen.

  name

  : The name of the operator, by default None. If None, the operator is not named and does not accept named inputs.

  remapping

  : A function that remaps the parameter theta, by default config.DEFAULT_MAPPING. To disable remapping, pass {code}`qw_map.none` or {code}`lambda x: x`.

classes.name: qandle.operators.RZ
documentation:
Parametrized RZ gate, i.e. a rotation around the z-axis of the Bloch sphere.

This class represents a parametrized RZ gate in a quantum circuit.

Used by {class}`qandle.qcircuit.QCircuit` to build the circuit.

Attributes
: qubit

  : The index of the qubit this gate operates on.

  theta

  : The parameter of the RZ gate, by default None. If None, a random parameter $[0, 1]$ is chosen.

  name

  : The name of the operator, by default None. If None, the operator is not named and does not accept named inputs.

  remapping

  : A function that remaps the parameter theta, by default config.DEFAULT_MAPPING. To disable remapping, pass {code}`qw_map.none` or {code}`lambda x: x`.

classes.name: qandle.operators.CNOT
documentation: CNOT gate.

classes.name: qandle.operators.CZ
documentation: CZ gate.
